app.title: iTop AI Assistant
app.token_gate_desc: The admin API rejected the request. Enter the admin token to continue.

layout.alert_body: The assistant is not configured yet — /webhook returns 503 until the LLM and iTop connections are set.

common.saved_next_ticket: Saved — applies from the next processed ticket
common.field_backend_url_desc: This assistant as reachable from the iTop server

setup.title: Setup
setup.configured: The assistant is configured — /webhook is active.
setup.security_copy_alert: Copy the tokens now — they are shown only this once. The admin token is saved to this browser automatically; the webhook token is written into the iTop webhook configuration at the iTop webhooks step (X-Auth-Token header).
setup.security_admin_token_desc: Bearer token for this UI and the /api endpoints
setup.itop_account_note: The account needs the REST Services User profile and is used as the AI service account — its comments must be distinguishable from engineers.
setup.webhooks_desc: Creates the triggers and webhooks in iTop that call this assistant on ticket creation and public log updates. Requires an iTop administrator account — the credentials are used for this one operation and are not stored anywhere. Existing objects are left untouched. This step is optional: the same objects can be created manually (see README) or with the CLI.
setup.llm_model_desc: Model name as exposed by the endpoint
setup.final_complete_detail: If you ran the iTop webhooks step, iTop is already calling {{origin}}/webhook. If you skipped it, configure the triggers and webhooks manually (see README) or from the Connections screen — the webhook token goes in the X-Auth-Token header and the trigger context must exclude REST/JSON.

connections.title: Connections
connections.reset_confirm: Reset the "{{section}}" section to env/yaml defaults?
connections.webhooks_desc: Creates the triggers and webhooks in iTop that call this assistant (uses the saved webhook token). Requires an iTop administrator account — the credentials are used once and never stored. Existing objects are left untouched.
connections.security_desc: Tokens are write-only: the current values are never shown. Use the generate buttons to create a strong random token, then copy it before saving.
connections.security_webhook_token_desc: Set the same value in the iTop Remote Application Connection (X-Auth-Token header)
connections.security_admin_token_desc: Bearer token for this UI and the /api endpoints; saved to this browser automatically
connections.clear_admin_token_confirm: Clear the admin token? The admin API becomes open to anyone who can reach it.
connections.clear_webhook_token_confirm: Clear the webhook token? /webhook will accept unauthenticated requests.
connections.llm_model_desc: Model name as exposed by the endpoint
connections.llm_think_tags_desc: Tag names stripped as inline reasoning blocks
connections.embeddings_model_desc: Embedding model as exposed by the endpoint; must be multilingual, e.g. bge-m3
connections.embeddings_dimension_mismatch: The endpoint returned dimension {{actual}} but the config says {{expected}} — fix the Dimension field
connections.ticket_mapping_desc: Maps semantic ticket fields onto the iTop datamodel: fields (semantic name → attribute code, null = attribute absent), class_overrides (per-class differences), active_statuses (when the assistant may act).

modules.title: Modules
modules.saved: Saved — applies from the next processed ticket
modules.reset_confirm: Reset the "{{name}}" config to env/yaml defaults?

prompts.title: Prompts
prompts.saved: Saved — applies from the next processed ticket
prompts.discard_confirm: Discard unsaved changes to the current prompt?
prompts.reset_confirm: Reset prompt "{{name}}" to the built-in default?

runs.title: Runs
runs.no_runs_filtered: No runs recorded for this filter.
runs.select_run: Select a run to see its steps.

vector.title: Vector index
vector.fingerprint_mismatch: The index was built with a different embeddings model or dimension — run a full reindex.
vector.index_none: No active index yet — it is created on the first sweep.
vector.reindex_confirm: Rebuild the entire index? Every object will be re-embedded — this can take a while and load the embeddings endpoint.
vector.field_index_values_desc: Values of the class's relevance attribute (e.g. status) that keep an object in the index; empty = index every object
vector.field_profile_desc: JSON: chunk kind → list of semantic fields
vector.field_enabled_desc: Applies from the next sweep, no restart needed
vector.field_env_desc: Isolates deployments sharing one Postgres (e.g. staging/prod)
